title: Citrus Pickled Venezuelan Chicken Pasta
description: A creamy, citrusy pasta dish featuring pickled cucumber, semolina flour, and tender chicken for a high-protein, datenight Venezuelan flavor profile.
instructions: Season the diced chicken with salt, pepper, cumin, and smoked paprika and let it rest for 10 minutes.
In a medium skillet, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat and sauté the chicken until browned and cooked through, about 6–8 minutes. Transfer chicken to a bowl and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil and sauté the diced pickled cucumber for 1–2 minutes until slightly softened.
Reduce the heat to low, pour in the heavy cream and stir until gently heated.
Stir in the Greek yogurt, orange juice, lime juice, and whole‑grain mustard until the sauce is smooth and creamy. Season with additional salt and pepper as needed.
Add the cooked chicken back into the sauce, let it simmer for 3 minutes to meld the flavors.
Meanwhile, cook the semolina flour in a separate pot according to package instructions, typically 3–4 minutes, until it forms a smooth, thick paste. Drain excess liquid.
Fold the cooked semolina into the creamy chicken sauce, stirring until well combined and the mixture is thick and silky.
Serve the pasta hot, garnished with a sprinkle of extra smoked paprika or fresh cilantro if desired.
If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of warm water or additional orange juice to reach desired consistency.
